text: The surgeon performs a transabdominal preperitoneal robotic repair, entering the abdomen to find the omentum incarcerated within the hernia defect. After reducing the omentum over fifteen to twenty minutes, the surgeon begins pre-pectineal dissection and divides the falciform ligament. The hernia sac is successfully reduced in its entirety.

The surgeon performs a circumferential pre-peritoneal dissection to facilitate mesh placement, encountering a more adherent peritoneum to the diaphragm than usual. A large amount of fat is observed and reduced from the left side of the hernia, necessitating a larger dissection due to bilateral defects.

The surgeon performs a preperitoneal dissection to prepare for mesh placement, followed by closing the hernia defects using running absorbable OV-lock sutures. The pneumoperitoneum is decreased to reduce tension on the closure. A barred soft preperitoneal macroporous polypropylene mesh is placed, and the peritoneal closure is completed to exclude the mesh from the peritoneal cavity.
